behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Rename detection is a real Git behavior (similarity thresholds, status formatting, and rename metadata) crossing filesystem + Git subprocess boundaries. A pure in-process mock of diff output can miss real rename classification failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real Git repo, commit a file, then rename it (`git mv`) and make a small edit. Run the CLI exactly as in production and assert stdout report has 1 rename, 0 add, 0 delete for that change, and the renamed path appears only in the rename category.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling often fails at real subprocess/output decoding and Git binary patch/report formats. Mocked text fixtures can hide decode/encoding and command-output edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real Git repo, add and commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes/image), then modify or add another binary file. Run the CLI and assert exit code is success, no traceback on stderr, and binary changes are reflected in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The no-commit case depends on real repository state (unborn HEAD) and Git command behavior/errors. Mocking repo APIs can miss how the tool handles actual Git responses in this state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new temporary Git repo with no commits. Run the CLI against it and assert successful completion with an empty structured report (zero counts / empty sections) and no error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real Git history semantics across multiple commits and change types. In-process mocks can trivially mirror expected values and miss parsing/selection errors from actual Git output.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known operations across commits (add, modify, rename, delete). Run the CLI for the target revision/window and compare reported category counts to counts independently computed from `git diff --name-status`/`git log --name-status` over the same scope.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior depends on real Git index and working-tree interactions, which are OS/filesystem + Git state-machine concerns. Mocked repos can hide index/worktree discrepancies.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real repo, create equivalent change sets twice: once staged (`git add`) and once unstaged. Run the CLI in each state with the same analysis target and assert normalized stdout reports are byte-for-byte identical.
